note: orchestrator class-alignment + Le Cam fix (D0.5 revise)
directive: def:law-class NOW INCLUDES ass:marginal-overlap and ass:strict-overlap-endpoint, so the upper bound (oeq:feasible-upper) and the two-sided bracket (thm:rate-characterization) hold over the FULL def:law-class P_class — drop any subclass-restriction language. RE-PROVE lem:witness-membership to verify the two-point witness lies in the enlarged class: it satisfies ass:marginal-overlap (rare-block mass c_B h^alpha <= C (q_n)^{1/gamma} with q_n=h^{beta_ag} and (1+alpha)beta_ag/gamma chosen by def:exponents) and ass:strict-overlap-endpoint (at gamma=0 the witness propensity is 1/4 on B_n and 1/2 off, both >= underline_p=1/4). DISCHARGE lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq as a CITED-CLASSICAL node whose proof IS the citation: Tsybakov (2009), Introduction to Nonparametric Estimation, Ch. 2 (two-point / Le Cam method), and Bretagnolle-Huber; state the exact two-point testing bound. thm:minimax-lower cites it instead of re-deriving the testing inequality.

note: D0.5 math fix: correct the upper-bound to the honest GLOBAL-drift form
directive: CORRECT the oeq:feasible-upper proof to the honest derivable form (the localized PRODUCT drift term is NOT derivable from L2 nuisance bounds and must be dropped): (1) The estimated-nuisance drift enters regret GLOBALLY/ADDITIVELY, bounded by E|b_q| <= r_mu_n r_e_n/q + r_mu_n q^{1/(2 gamma)} via Cauchy-Schwarz (L1) from lem:clip-bias; do NOT multiply it by the margin factor R^{alpha/(2+2 alpha)} (||b_q||_2 cannot carry the r_mu r_e product from L2 bounds on each factor). (2) The STOCHASTIC term uses the AIPW conditional VARIANCE Var(Gamma_q | X) <= C/max{p,q} <= C/q (the inverse-propensity score variance, floored by clipping), NOT the crude envelope q^{-1}; this gives localized radius n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2} R^{alpha/(2+2 alpha)} and stochastic exponent A_alpha(1-s) with A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). (3) Balancing q=n^{-s}: r_feas=min{r_star, g}, g=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)} (c=a_mu+a_e the product exponent, a=a_mu). This matches def:feasible-rate as corrected. Update lem:feasible-master-bound / lem:clip-balance-exponent accordingly (drop the kappa-power on the drift terms). The bracket thm:rate-characterization then holds with this honest g; the gap r_star - g is the genuine weak-overlap nuisance cost (tightness open, oeq:feasible-tight).

changed.from: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, the standing feasible-estimator conditions used in oeq:feasible-upper, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), and gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) and r_feas the derived plain-clipped-AIPW exponent. Moreover r_feas<=r_star, with equality at gamma=0 and more generally whenever kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma). The strict positive gap occurs precisely in the nuisance-limited weak-overlap branch kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma); feasible tightness is resolved in the equal-exponent branch and remains open in the strict-gap branch (oeq:feasible-tight).
changed.to: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, the standing feasible-estimator conditions used in oeq:feasible-upper, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), and gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) and r_feas=min{r_star,g(a,c,gamma)} for g(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)} when gamma>0, and r_feas(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}. Moreover r_feas<=r_star; equality holds exactly when g(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma), and at gamma=0 this is the condition c>=A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). The strict positive gap occurs precisely when g(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma); feasible tightness is resolved in the equal-exponent branch and remains open in the strict-gap branch (oeq:feasible-tight).
changed.reason: The current clause says equality at gamma=0 without the nuisance-rate side condition. But def:feasible-rate gives r_feas(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c} and r_star(alpha,0)=A_alpha, so equality can fail when c<A_alpha. The current statement also uses the undefined placeholder kappa zeta; the proof derives the balancing exponent g from def:feasible-rate. The proposed version narrows only the equality claim to the exact regime in which it is true and keeps the bracket unchanged.

changed.to: For the cross-fitted propensity estimator used in def:feasible-erm and each analysis clip q, with ehat_q(x)=min{1-q,max{q,ehat(x)}}, there is a constant C_v such that, conditionally on the training folds, e_P(x)/ehat_q(x)^2 + (1-e_P(x))/(1-ehat_q(x))^2 <= C_v/max{p_P(x),q} for P_X-almost every x.
changed.reason: The feasible upper proof needs the localized stochastic term to scale with the conditional variance of the clipped AIPW score. L2 nuisance rates alone do not prevent an estimated clipped propensity from creating a larger inverse-propensity variance on an evaluation fold.

note: D0.5 math fix round-3: complete the upper-bound proof spine
directive: Complete oeq:feasible-upper with a FULLY-DECLARED proof spine (the reviewer enumerated the gaps): (1) The ERM basic inequality has the empirical-process term sup_pi |(P_n - P)[(pi - pi_star)(tau_P + b_q)]|; INCLUDE it. The tau_P part is the localized policy-learning stochastic term controlled by thm:margin-localization + ass:vc-localized-envelope, giving the oracle rate n^{-r_star}; the centered clipped-score part gives (n q)^{-A_alpha} with A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha); do not drop either. (2) Var(Gamma_q|X) <= C/max{p_P,q} now follows from ass:clipped-propensity-variance-stability (declared) + ass:bounded-outcome — use it for the n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2} localized radius. (3) DECLARE every helper lemma as a core added_lemma node with its own proof_tex AND wire it into oeq:feasible-upper.depends_on (no undeclared proof spine): lem:clipped-score-drift-bound (E|b_q| <= C(r_mu_n r_e_n/q + r_mu_n q^{1/(2 gamma)}) via lem:clip-bias + Cauchy-Schwarz, GLOBAL/additive — not localized), lem:clipped-score-variance-envelope (Var(Gamma_q|X) <= C/max{p_P,q}), lem:feasible-master-bound (the ERM self-bound assembling the three stochastic/drift terms into R <= n^{-r_star} + (n q)^{-A_alpha} + E|b_q|), lem:clip-balance-exponent (g(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}). (4) r_feas = min{r_star, g} per def:feasible-rate; the symbol layer no longer uses kappa_mu. The Le Cam leaf lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq is a CITED-CLASSICAL node (Tsybakov 2009 / Bretagnolle-Huber) discharged by citation; do not re-derive it.

changed.from: Let A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For certified polynomial nuisance exponents a,c with r_mu_n=O(n^{-a}) and r_mu_n r_e_n=O(n^{-c}), c>=1/2: the clipped-AIPW ERM welfare regret is bounded by the GLOBAL (additive, NON-localized) estimated-nuisance drift r_mu_n r_e_n/q + r_mu_n q^{1/(2 gamma)} plus a localized stochastic term whose radius uses the AIPW conditional VARIANCE Var(Gamma_q|X) <~ 1/max{p,q} (scale n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2}), NOT the crude envelope q^{-1}. Balancing the clip q=n^{-s} gives g(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)} and r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma), g(a,c,gamma)}. At gamma=0 (strict-overlap floor, q fixed) r_feas=min{A_alpha, c}, which equals r_star=A_alpha when c>=A_alpha. The drift is GLOBAL because ||b_q||_2 cannot carry the r_mu r_e product from L2 bounds on each factor; only E|b_q| (Cauchy-Schwarz, L1) gives the product, and that enters regret additively, not through the margin self-bound.
changed.to: For gamma>0, fix q_0 in (0,min{1/2,c_o}] and use analysis clips q_n=q_0 n^{-s}; if the optimizer exists let s_feas be an argmax of min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}, otherwise let s_n be any o(1)-optimal maximizing sequence and use q_n=q_0 n^{-s_n}. Define g(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)} and r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),g(a,c,gamma)}. For gamma=0, use a fixed q_0<=underline_p/2 and set r_feas(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}=min{r_star(alpha,0),c}.
changed.reason: This fills the OEQ construction handle with the explicit clip schedule, including the nonattained-supremum case and the strict-overlap endpoint, while preserving the corrected rate functional from the frozen core.

directive: DELETE ass:clipped-propensity-variance-stability. Keep estimated cross-fitted propensity in def:feasible-erm with ONLY ass:nuisance-rate. Headline thm:rate-characterization on the sharp lower bound (already proven via thm:minimax-lower). Achievability: DERIVE (never assert) the honest upper exponent r_up from the crude clipping variance envelope (q^{-2}, weights <= 1/q) + deterministic lem:clip-bias controlled by ass:overlap-decay + ass:margin. State matched where r_up = r_star; leave oeq:feasible-tight as the explicit open residual where r_up > r_star. Drop the redundant overlap clause; collapse to ONE class if the cruder achievability holds over def:law-class, and retire def:feasible-law-class. NO rate-buying, NO known propensity, NO assumed cancellation, NO oracle.

changed.from: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, the standing feasible-estimator conditions used in oeq:feasible-upper, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), and gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) and r_feas=min{r_star,g(a,c,gamma)} for g(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)} when gamma>0, and r_feas(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}. Moreover r_feas<=r_star; equality holds exactly when g(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma), and at gamma=0 this is the condition c>=A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). The strict positive gap occurs precisely when g(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma); feasible tightness is resolved in the equal-exponent branch and remains open in the strict-gap branch (oeq:feasible-tight).
changed.to: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents, ass:vc-localized-envelope, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), and gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_up(alpha,gamma,a,c)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) and, for gamma>0, r_up=min{r_star,g_crude(a,c,gamma)} where g_crude(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}. At gamma=0, r_up(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}. Moreover r_up<=r_star; equality holds exactly when g_crude(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma), and at gamma=0 this is the condition c>=A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). The strict positive gap occurs precisely when g_crude(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma); feasible tightness is resolved in the equal-exponent branch and remains open in the strict-gap branch (oeq:feasible-tight).
changed.reason: The current feasible upper exponent uses the q^{-1} localized variance scale that required the rejected ass:clipped-propensity-variance-stability. With only the declared bounded-score consequence of clipping, |Gamma_q|<=C/q and the centered score variance scale is q^{-2}, changing the stochastic exponent from A_alpha(1-s) to A_alpha(1-2s). This is a narrowing because g_crude<=g and hence r_up<=r_feas.

changed.from: For gamma>0, fix q_0 in (0,min{1/2,c_o}] and use analysis clips q_n=q_0 n^{-s}; if the optimizer exists let s_feas be an argmax of min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}, otherwise let s_n be any o(1)-optimal maximizing sequence and use q_n=q_0 n^{-s_n}. Define g(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)} and r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),g(a,c,gamma)}. For gamma=0, use a fixed q_0<=underline_p/2 and set r_feas(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}=min{r_star(alpha,0),c}.
changed.to: For gamma>0, fix q_0 in (0,min{1/2,c_o}] and use analysis clips q_n=q_0 n^{-s}. Define g_crude(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}; the supremum is attained, and s_feas may be chosen as any maximizer. Define the feasible exponent under the plain cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM without propensity-variance stability by r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),g_crude(a,c,gamma)}. For gamma=0, use a fixed q_0<=underline_p/2 and set r_feas(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}=min{r_star(alpha,0),c}.
changed.reason: The old construction encoded the stronger q^{-1} variance scale. After deleting ass:clipped-propensity-variance-stability, the derivable score envelope is |Gamma_q|<=C/q, so the localized stochastic term is (n q^2)^{-A_alpha}; the rate functional must therefore use A_alpha(1-2s).

changed.from: For the cross-fitted propensity estimator used in def:feasible-erm and each analysis clip q, with ehat_q(x)=min{1-q,max{q,ehat(x)}}, there is a constant C_v such that, conditionally on the training folds, e_P(x)/ehat_q(x)^2 + (1-e_P(x))/(1-ehat_q(x))^2 <= C_v/max{p_P(x),q} for P_X-almost every x.
changed.reason: Rejected as rate-buying: it assumes estimated propensity multiplicative stability near weak-overlap boundary and buys the forbidden q^{-1} variance scale.

changed.from: Use lem:clip-bias's EXACT identity (no assumed cancellation). Bound the policy-weighted drift: |E[(pi-pi_star) b_q]| <= ||ebar_q-e_P||_2 ||Delta/ebar_q||_2. Split ||ebar_q-e_P||_2 <= ||ehat-e||_2 (=r_e_n, off-clip DR product) + ||ehat-ebar_q||_2 (clip displacement, supported on {p<=q}, <= C q * P{p<=q}^{1/2} <= C q^{1+1/(2gamma)} by ass:marginal-overlap); and ||Delta/ebar_q||_2 <= r_mu_n/q (clip floor). So clip bias B <= r_mu_n r_e_n / q + r_mu_n q^{1/(2gamma)}. Variance A = (q^{-1} rho^{alpha/(1+alpha)}/n)^{1/2} (ass:vc-localized-envelope + thm:margin-localization). Self-bound R <= C(A+B)^{(2+2alpha)/(2+alpha)}; MINIMIZE over q; DERIVE the closed-form r_feas(alpha,gamma,r_mu_n,r_e_n) (your output, proof must match) and reduce at gamma=0. The 1/q-amplified DR product is the weak-overlap (Dorn) effect; keep it, do not cancel it.
changed.to: Use lem:clip-bias exactly and do not assume denominator stability, cancellation, known propensity, or oracle weights. The stochastic term must use only the crude feasible clipped-score envelope |Gamma_q| <= C/q, hence second moment/envelope scale q^{-2}; any q^{-1} variance or shell-variance scale from e_P/ehat_q^2 is forbidden unless derived from the primitive nuisance assumptions, and it is not available here. Combine this crude empirical-process term with the deterministic clip-bias terms controlled by ass:overlap-decay and ass:margin, then optimize q=n^{-s}. The intended rate functional is the honest crude exponent already encoded in def:feasible-rate, g_crude(a,c,gamma)=sup_s min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}, capped by r_star for the bracket. If any branch cannot be closed over def:law-class, state the remaining gap only as oeq:feasible-tight; do not introduce a smaller feasible-law class or a new estimator-side variance assumption.
changed.reason: Invalidate stale q^{-1} upper route; force crude q^{-2} feasible-envelope achievability and leave tightness open where loose.

directive: ROUND 5 SUPERSEDES rounds 1-3 and all stale auto-apply entries for the feasible upper branch. It is forbidden to reintroduce ass:clipped-propensity-variance-stability, q^{-1} shell variance for estimated propensities, def:feasible-law-class, known propensity, oracle denominator, or assumed cancellation. Re-solve only the honest crude q^{-2} feasible upper branch over def:law-class, or leave the residual as oeq:feasible-tight.

changed.from: P{p_P(X) <= v} <= C_om v^(1/gamma) for all 0 < v <= c_o, with the convention v^(1/gamma)=1 when gamma=0.
changed.reason: Assumption economy: the requested honest upper route must use the baseline joint overlap-decay plus margin, not a separate marginal overlap tail.

changed.from: { P : ass:iid, ass:consistency, ass:unconfoundedness, ass:bounded-outcome, ass:positivity, ass:margin, ass:zero-effect, ass:overlap-decay, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:strict-overlap-endpoint, ass:policy-class, and ass:optimal-in-class hold at exponents (alpha,gamma) }
changed.to: { P : ass:iid, ass:consistency, ass:unconfoundedness, ass:bounded-outcome, ass:positivity, ass:margin, ass:zero-effect, ass:overlap-decay, ass:strict-overlap-endpoint, ass:policy-class, and ass:optimal-in-class hold at exponents (alpha,gamma) }

The stochastic term must use only the crude feasible clipped-score envelope |Gamma_q| <= C/q, hence second moment/envelope scale q^{-2}; any q^{-1} variance or shell-variance scale from e_P/ehat_q^2 is forbidden unless derived from the primitive nuisance assumptions, and it is not available here. Combine this crude empirical-process term with the deterministic clip-bias terms controlled by ass:overlap-decay and ass:margin, then optimize q=n^{-s}. The intended rate functional is the honest crude exponent already encoded in def:feasible-rate, g_crude(a,c,gamma)=sup_s min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}, capped by r_star for the bracket. If any branch cannot be closed over def:law-class, state the remaining gap only as oeq:feasible-tight; do not introduce a smaller feasible-law class or a new estimator-side variance assumption.
changed.to: Use lem:clip-bias exactly and do not assume denominator stability, cancellation, known propensity, oracle weights, or a separate marginal-overlap tail. The stochastic term must use only the crude feasible clipped-score envelope |Gamma_q| <= C/q, hence second moment/envelope scale q^{-2}. The intended rate functional remains the honest crude exponent encoded in def:feasible-rate, g_crude(a,c,gamma)=sup_s min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}, capped by r_star for the bracket. If the joint-overlap/margin route cannot close over def:law-class, state that residual only as oeq:feasible-tight; do not add a smaller law class or a new overlap assumption.
changed.reason: Force deterministic clip-bias control from ass:overlap-decay + ass:margin/localization; no marginal-overlap shortcut.

changed.from: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents, ass:vc-localized-envelope, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), and gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_up(alpha,gamma,a,c)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) and, for gamma>0, r_up=min{r_star,g_crude(a,c,gamma)} where g_crude(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}. At gamma=0, r_up(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}. The strict positive gap occurs precisely when g_crude(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma); feasible tightness is resolved in the equal-exponent branch and remains open in the strict-gap branch (oeq:feasible-tight).

At gamma=0, r_up(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}. changed.reason: Remove redundant ass:marginal-overlap from the headline theorem assumptions.

directive: ROUND 6 MAXIMALITY/ASSUMPTION-ECONOMY DIRECTIVE: ass:marginal-overlap is deleted. Do not reintroduce a marginal overlap tail or any smaller law class. If the crude q^{-2} feasible upper branch cannot be derived from def:law-class using ass:overlap-decay + ass:margin/localization + lem:clip-bias, leave that limitation as oeq:feasible-tight rather than strengthening assumptions.

changed.from: Under ass:margin-window, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents, ass:vc-localized-envelope, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), and gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_up(alpha,gamma,a,c)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) and, for gamma>0, r_up=min{r_star,g_crude(a,c,gamma)} where g_crude(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}. At gamma=0, r_up(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}. Moreover r_up<=r_star; equality holds exactly when g_crude(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma), and at gamma=0 this is the condition c>=A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). The strict positive gap occurs precisely when g_crude(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma); feasible tightness is resolved in the equal-exponent branch and remains open in the strict-gap branch (oeq:feasible-tight).
changed.to: Under the same assumptions, c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_up(alpha,gamma,a,c)}(log n)^p, where for gamma>0, r_up=min{r_star,g_joint} and g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)=sup_{s>=0,0<=t<=s/gamma} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)+alpha t/2, 2a-t}. At gamma=0, r_up(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}. Equality with r_star holds exactly when g_joint>=r_star for gamma>0, and exactly when c>=A_alpha at gamma=0; the strict-gap branch g_joint<r_star remains oeq:feasible-tight.
changed.reason: After ass:marginal-overlap was removed, the term r_mu_n q^{1/(2 gamma)} is not derivable globally. The high-contrast weak-overlap part is controlled only through disagreement regret and Young's inequality, producing the additional optimized localization split t and the term 2a-t. The old exponent can be too fast in regimes with slow outcome-regression learning, so the theorem must use the corrected honest exponent.

changed.from: For gamma>0, fix q_0 in (0,min{1/2,c_o}] and use analysis clips q_n=q_0 n^{-s}. Define g_crude(a,c,gamma)=sup_{s>=0} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)}; the supremum is attained, and s_feas may be chosen as any maximizer. Define the feasible exponent under the plain cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM without propensity-variance stability by r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),g_crude(a,c,gamma)}. For gamma=0, use a fixed q_0<=underline_p/2 and set r_feas(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}=min{r_star(alpha,0),c}.
changed.to: Let A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0, fix u_bar in (0,u_0] and q_0 in (0,min{1/2,c_o u_bar^gamma}]. For q_n=q_0 n^{-s} and proof-localization split u_n=u_bar n^{-t}, require 0<=t<=s/gamma. Define g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)=sup_{s>=0,0<=t<=s/gamma} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)+alpha t/2, 2a-t}. The supremum is attained; choose s_feas as the first coordinate of any maximizer and use q_n=q_0 n^{-s_feas}. Define r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)}. For gamma=0, use fixed q_0<=underline_p/2 and set r_feas(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}.
changed.reason: The old construction encoded the marginal-overlap shortcut P(p_P<=q)^{1/2}=O(q^{1/(2 gamma)}), which is no longer a member property of def:law-class. From the joint overlap-decay condition one obtains only a localized bound P(D_pi intersect {p_P<=q})<=C u^alpha q^{1/gamma}+R_P(pi)/u. Balancing this bound adds the proof-only split t and the Young term 2a-t. The corrected definition is the computable rate functional actually derived from the frozen assumptions.

changed.reason: Law-class should contain law restrictions, not finite-VC policy-class or optimal-in-class richness; those are theorem/estimator-side conditions.

changed.from: For all large n the corrected witness laws P_{n,+}, P_{n,-} satisfy ass:iid, ass:consistency, ass:unconfoundedness, ass:bounded-outcome, ass:positivity, ass:margin, ass:zero-effect, and ass:overlap-decay. If, in addition, the two explicit witness-optimal policies x -> 1 and x -> 1{x notin B_n} belong to Pi, then P_{n,+}, P_{n,-} lie in def:law-class P_class(alpha,gamma). (Normalization: the margin window satisfies u_0<2, so an off-block contrast tau_0 in (u_0, 2) exists under bounded outcomes |Y|<=1.)
changed.to: For all large n the corrected witness laws P_{n,+}, P_{n,-} satisfy ass:iid, ass:consistency, ass:unconfoundedness, ass:bounded-outcome, ass:positivity, ass:margin, ass:zero-effect, ass:overlap-decay, and ass:strict-overlap-endpoint, hence P_{n,+}, P_{n,-} lie in def:law-class P_class(alpha,gamma). (Normalization: the margin window satisfies u_0<2, so an off-block contrast tau_0 in (u_0, 2) exists under bounded outcomes |Y|<=1.)

changed.from: CONSTRUCT-AND-DETERMINE. Assume gamma<=alpha+1, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:vc-localized-envelope, ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, and ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents, over the baseline class def:law-class. For the plain cross-fit clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm with clip q chosen by the analysis, DERIVE (do NOT pre-specify) the best honest worst-case welfare-regret exponent r_up obtainable using only the crude clipped-score envelope from weights bounded by 1/q (second-moment scale q^{-2}) plus deterministic lem:clip-bias controlled by ass:overlap-decay and ass:margin. State the clip q and resulting r_up in closed form, compare to r_star, and leave oeq:feasible-tight open where r_up<r_star.
changed.to: CONDITIONAL ACHIEVABILITY / CONSTRUCT-AND-DETERMINE. Over the baseline class def:law-class, fix a cross-fitted nuisance-estimator sequence satisfying ass:nuisance-rate, ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, and ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents, and assume ass:vc-localized-envelope for Pi. For the plain cross-fit clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm with deterministic clip q chosen by the analysis, derive the honest conditional worst-case welfare-regret exponent r_up obtainable using only the crude clipped-score envelope from weights bounded by 1/q (second-moment scale q^{-2}) plus deterministic lem:clip-bias controlled by ass:overlap-decay and ass:margin/localization. This is an estimator-side conditional achievability bound, not a claim that the nuisance rates are implied by def:law-class.
changed.reason: Make the feasible upper an explicitly conditional achievability theorem under estimator-side nuisance rates, not an unconditional minimax upper over def:law-class.

changed.from: Under the same assumptions, c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_up(alpha,gamma,a,c)}(log n)^p, where for gamma>0, r_up=min{r_star,g_joint} and g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)=sup_{s>=0,0<=t<=s/gamma} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)+alpha t/2, 2a-t}. At gamma=0, r_up(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}. Equality with r_star holds exactly when g_joint>=r_star for gamma>0, and exactly when c>=A_alpha at gamma=0; the strict-gap branch g_joint<r_star remains oeq:feasible-tight.
changed.to: Sharp lower-bound characterization. Assume ass:margin-window and that the two explicit witness-optimal policies from def:two-point-witness, x -> 1 and x -> 1{x notin B_n}, belong to Pi for all large n. Then M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} for all large n, where r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}). The feasible clipped-AIPW achievability calculation is the separate conditional node oeq:feasible-upper, with exponent r_up; equality of the conditional upper exponent with r_star holds only in the equal-exponent branch, while the strict-gap branch remains oeq:feasible-tight.
changed.reason: Headline the sharp lower bound; cite the conditional upper separately and leave strict-gap feasible tightness open.

directive: ROUND 7 D0.5 RESPONSE: Prove or explicitly derive the standard testing/product-chi-square and localized-ERM/self-bound leaves in-core; citation-only leaves are not accepted. Do not claim nuisance rates are implied by def:law-class. Keep thm:rate-characterization as the sharp lower-bound headline plus pointer to conditional achievability, not a same-class matched minimax theorem.

Prove witness membership only from def:two-point-witness, def:law-class, prop:overlap-envelope, and primitive assumptions. Keep the lower-bound dependency direction lem:witness-membership -> thm:minimax-lower -> thm:rate-characterization.

The two explicit witness-optimal policies are x -> 1 and x -> 1{x notin B_n}; if these policies belong to Pi, the lower-bound reduction in thm:minimax-lower applies. (Normalization: the margin window satisfies u_0<2, so an off-block contrast tau_0 in (u_0, 2) exists under bounded outcomes |Y|<=1.)

changed.reason: Make the headline theorem a lower-bound-only corollary, with no dependency on feasible achievability or open residual nodes.
Make the open residual compare thm:minimax-lower with oeq:feasible-upper without depending on thm:rate-characterization.

directive: ROUND 10 CYCLE REPAIR: thm:rate-characterization is lower-bound-only and may depend only on thm:minimax-lower and def:exponents. It must NOT mention or depend on oeq:feasible-upper or oeq:feasible-tight. oeq:feasible-tight is a separate open residual comparing thm:minimax-lower with the conditional oeq:feasible-upper; it must NOT depend on thm:rate-characterization and must never be an input to the headline theorem.

changed.reason: Remove redundant witness-policy membership premise; the Le Cam proof uses arbitrary Pi-valued outputs and in-class witness laws.
Inherit the lower-bound-only theorem without the redundant policy-richness premise.

directive: ROUND 11 D0.5 RESPONSE: Remove redundant witness-optimal-policy membership from thm:minimax-lower and thm:rate-characterization; the lower proof needs in-class witness laws and regret separation for arbitrary Pi-valued decisions, not that the two witness-optimal policies lie in Pi. Any node invoking thm:welfare-identity must carry ass:bounded-outcome explicitly. The feasible upper branch is a conditional U_n bound: lem:crude-localized-master-bound and oeq:feasible-upper must surface all consumed conditions (def:law-class plus ass:optimal-in-class, nuisance-rate, bounded-crossfit-nuisances, polynomial nuisance exponents, VC-localized envelope, overlap/margin/localization, bounded outcome). Do not reintroduce clipped-propensity-variance-stability, marginal-overlap, feasible-law-class, known propensity, or an unconditional minimax upper claim.

directive: ROUND 12 D0.5 RESPONSE: The D0.R def:feasible-law-class split is rejected. Do not create or depend on def:feasible-law-class. Keep U_n as a conditional upper risk over def:law-class plus explicit side conditions (ass:optimal-in-class, nuisance-rate, bounded-crossfit-nuisances, polynomial nuisance exponents, ass:policy-class, ass:vc-localized-envelope). Accept the faithful estimator repair: def:feasible-erm is a measurable countable-skeleton 1/n ERM, with lem:feasible-erm-basic-inequality proving the comparator inequality. NO clipped-propensity-variance-stability, NO marginal-overlap, NO feasible-law-class, NO known propensity, NO oracle denominator, NO unconditional minimax upper claim.

changed.from: Let A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0, fix u_bar in (0,u_0] and q_0 in (0,min{1/2,c_o u_bar^gamma}]. For q_n=q_0 n^{-s} and proof-localization split u_n=u_bar n^{-t}, require 0<=t<=s/gamma. Define g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)=sup_{s>=0,0<=t<=s/gamma} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)+alpha t/2, 2a-t}. The supremum is attained; choose s_feas as the first coordinate of any maximizer and use q_n=q_0 n^{-s_feas}. Define r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)}. For gamma=0, use fixed q_0<=underline_p/2 and set r_feas(alpha,0)=min{A_alpha,c}.
changed.to: Let A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0, fix u_bar in (0,u_0] and q_0 in (0,min{1/2,c_o u_bar^gamma}]. For q_n=q_0 n^{-s} and localization split u_n=u_bar n^{-t}, require 0<=t<=s/gamma. Define g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)=sup_{s>=0,0<=t<=s/gamma} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)+alpha t/2, 2a-t}. Choose an attaining maximizer (s_feas,t_feas), set q_n=q_0 n^{-s_feas} and u_n=u_bar n^{-t_feas}, and define the OEQ feasible upper exponent r_up=r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)}. For gamma=0, use fixed q_n=q_0<=underline_p/2 and define r_up=r_feas=min{A_alpha,c}.
changed.reason: The OEQ handle asks for the derived estimator tuning and exponent. The frozen formula already contains the correct crude q^{-2} balance; this correction explicitly names the localization coordinate t_feas, the analysis sequence u_n used in the proof, and r_up as the constructed exponent proved for U_n.
Accept regime-indexed schedule, balanced K-fold 1/n ERM, and conditional U_n scope.
Fixed-radius localized process bound as explicit DAG node.
Pooled cross-fit empirical-process bridge as explicit DAG node.
Use one pooled self-localized inequality including centered terms and add n^{-r_star} directly rather than by false domination.

directive: ROUND 13 D0.5 RESPONSE: Accept the latest D0.R repair only insofar as it keeps one baseline def:law-class and makes the feasible upper a regime-indexed conditional U_n bound. Persist def:feasible-rate with one fixed nuisance regime and deterministic schedule; def:feasible-erm as balanced K-fold countable-skeleton 1/n ERM; def:upper-risk as supremum over def:law-class plus explicit side conditions. Add lem:localized-vc-process-bound and lem:crossfit-localized-process-reduction as in-DAG lemmas. In lem:crude-localized-master-bound use a single pooled centered cross-fit process around foldwise conditional means, include all centered terms, absorb the drift sqrt(R/u) term before applying the generic self-bound, and include n^{-r_star} as a direct oracle-process add-on, not by claiming domination by (n q^2)^(-A_alpha). Do not create any new law-class split, marginal-tail shortcut, clipped-propensity variance-stability premise, known propensity, oracle denominator, or unconditional minimax upper claim.

directive: ROUND 14 D0 GATED RESPONSE: Reject the proposed narrowing of lem:localized-vc-self-bound as an unproved offset premise. Do not assume the random-radius/offset control inside the upper proof. Instead, make the standard localized VC offset-positive-part control explicit in ass:vc-localized-envelope and add lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control as a DAG lemma deriving the pooled cross-fit offset bound under ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count, ass:vc-localized-envelope, and thm:margin-localization. lem:localized-vc-self-bound should be only deterministic algebra from an already-proved offset-control node. Accept ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count as faithful bookkeeping. Continue to forbid any new law-class split, marginal-tail shortcut, clipped-propensity variance-stability premise, known propensity, oracle denominator, or unconditional minimax upper claim.

The offset control is discharged by the explicit dependency lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control, which itself derives from ass:vc-localized-envelope and fixed-K cross-fitting. Treat lem:localized-vc-self-bound as deterministic algebra from that proved dependency. directive: ROUND 16 POST-SOLVE GATE FIX: Split the empirical-process assumptions atomically. ass:vc-localized-envelope is only the fixed-radius localized VC process bound. ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope is only the localized offset positive-part bound used by lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control. Do not merge them into one omnibus assumption. These are standard empirical-process side conditions for the conditional upper branch, not law-class restrictions and not rate-buying overlap/propensity assumptions.
